2605.03482.title: MEMSAD: Gradient-Coupled Anomaly Detection for Memory Poisoning in Retrieval-Augmented Agents
2605.03482.paper_summary: Persistent external memory enables LLM agents to maintain context across sessions, yet its security properties remain formally uncharacterized. We formalize memory poisoning attacks on retrieval-augmented agents as a Stackelberg game with a unified evaluation framework spanning three attack classes with escalating access assumptions. Correcting an evaluation protocol inconsistency in the triggered-query specification of Chen et al. (2024), we show faithful evaluation increases measured attack success by $4\times$ (ASR-R: $0.25 \to 1.00$). Our primary contribution is MEMSAD (Semantic Anomaly Detection), a calibration-based defense grounded in a gradient coupling theorem: under encoder regularity, the anomaly score gradient and the retrieval objective gradient are provably identical, so any continuous perturbation that reduces detection risk necessarily degrades retrieval rank. This coupling yields a certified detection radius guaranteeing correct classification regardless of adversary strategy. We prove minimax optimality via Le Cam's method, showing any threshold detector requires $Ω(1/ρ^2)$ calibration samples and MEMSAD achieves this up to $\log(1/δ)$ factors. We further derive online regret bounds for rolling calibration at rate $O(σ^{2/3}Δ^{1/3})$, and formally characterize a discrete synonym-invariance loophole that marks the boundary of what continuous-space defenses can guarantee. Experiments on a $3 \times 5$ attack-defense matrix with bootstrap confidence intervals, Bonferroni-corrected hypothesis tests, and Clopper-Pearson validation ($n=1{,}000$) confirm: composite defenses achieve TPR $= 1.00$, FPR $= 0.00$ across all attacks, while synonym substitution evades detection at $Δ$ ASR-R $\approx 0$, exposing a gap existing embedding-based defenses cannot close.
2605.03482.paper_summary_zh: 持久的外部記憶使得 LLM 代理能夠在會話之間保持上下文，但其安全性特徵仍然未被正式描述。我們將針對檢索增強代理的記憶中毒攻擊形式化為一個 Stackelberg 遊戲，並建立一個統一的評估框架，涵蓋三個具有逐步訪問假設的攻擊類別。糾正 Chen 等人 (2024) 在觸發查詢規範中的評估協議不一致，我們顯示忠實評估使得測量攻擊成功率提高了 $4\times$ (ASR-R: $0.25 \to 1.00$)。我們的主要貢獻是 MEMSAD（語義異常檢測），這是一種基於校準的防禦，基於一個梯度耦合定理：在編碼器正則性下，異常分數的梯度和檢索目標的梯度是可證明相同的，因此任何減少檢測風險的連續擾動必然會降低檢索排名。這種耦合產生了一個認證的檢測半徑，保證無論對手策略如何均可正確分類。我們通過 Le Cam 方法證明了最小最大最優性，顯示任何閾值檢測器需要 $Ω(1/ρ^2)$ 的校準樣本，而 MEMSAD 在 $\log(1/δ)$ 因子內達到這一點。我們進一步推導了以速率 $O(σ^{2/3}Δ^{1/3})$ 進行滾動校準的在線後悔界限，並正式描述了一個離散同義詞不變的漏洞，標誌著連續空間防禦能夠保證的邊界。在一個 $3 \times 5$ 的攻擊-防禦矩陣上進行的實驗，配合自助信心區間、Bonferroni 修正的假設檢驗和 Clopper-Pearson 驗證 ($n=1{,}000$) 確認：復合防禦在所有攻擊中達到 TPR $= 1.00$，FPR $= 0.00$，而同義詞替換在 $Δ$ ASR-R $\approx 0$ 時逃避檢測，暴露出現有基於嵌入的防禦無法彌補的差距。
2605.03482.author: Ishrith Gowda et.al.
